{"society_power": 8.0, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 1.5, "key_event": "Civil War and federal state-building", "reasoning": "The Civil War dramatically expanded federal state capacity through conscription, income tax, national banking, war debt, and military occupation of the South, pushing the state toward \"strong.\" Society remained vigorous through mass mobilization and emancipated Black civil society, but wartime coercion, habeas corpus suspension, and violent sectional conflict modestly reduced its net constraining capacity."}
